judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports hit all four expectations with equivalent, well-cited findings; they are comparable.

report_1.evidence: New refund calls should live in `medibill/backend/app/billing/gateway.py` as a new accessor ... `medibill/backend/app/api/invoices.py:14-16` — the `POST /invoices/{invoice_id}/charge` route constructs its own `chargekit.Client` ... `medibill/backend/app/workers/dunning.py:9-13` — the dunning retry worker constructs a fresh `chargekit.Client` ... `app/billing/errors.py` as the sole exception (it only re-exports SDK exception types

report_2.evidence: New refund calls should be a thin accessor in `medibill/backend/app/billing/gateway.py` ... `medibill/backend/app/api/invoices.py:4` imports `chargekit` and `invoices.py:14-16` constructs its own `chargekit.Client(...)` ... `medibill/backend/app/workers/dunning.py:2` imports `chargekit` and `dunning.py:9-13` constructs a fresh `chargekit.Client(...)` ... naming `app/billing/errors.py` as the only exception (it imports only the SDK's error classes

report_1.evidence: `InvoiceOut` in `models/invoice.py:16-22` now includes a sixth field, `external_ref`, populated by the charge route (`api/invoices.py:17`) and backed by migration `0006_add_invoice_external_ref.sql`

report_2.evidence: The actual response model `InvoiceOut` in `medibill/backend/app/models/invoice.py:16-22` has a sixth field, `external_ref`, returned by the charge route (`invoices.py:18`). The field was added to the schema by `backend/migrations/0006_add_invoice_external_ref.sql`

report_1.evidence: It should be updated **as part of the refund feature's first change, before or alongside any new code** — not after. Concretely, in the same PR ... Updating it "later" is how it got three claims out of date across just two follow-up commits; the doc should ship in lockstep with the code that invalidates it

report_2.evidence: The drift should be treated as a bug and fixed **before or in the same commit as the first refund code**, not as a later docs cleanup ... any refund change that touches the API shape ... or the webhook handling must update this doc in the same commit

report_1.evidence: drop the hard-coded count entirely. A literal "handles N event types" is the most fragile possible phrasing — it went stale on the very next commit that touched the file. State the *behavior* instead ... See `app/api/webhooks.py` for the authoritative list.

report_2.evidence: a hard-coded count is guaranteed to go stale again (it already has ...). Restate it without a number, pointing at the source of truth, e.g.: "Handled webhook event types are enumerated in `backend/app/api/webhooks.py`
